title: Electroporation of COS-7 cells and functionalization of QDs
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"bf0ds","text":"Detailed cell electroporation protocol from paper:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fi6t2","text":"Probing cytoskeletal modulation of passive and active intracellular dynamics using nanobody-functionalized quantum dots\nEugene A Katrukha, Marina Mikhaylova, Hugo X van Brakel, Paul M van Bergen en Henegouwen, Anna Akhmanova, Casper C Hoogenraad, Lukas C Kapitein","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":119}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"efa15","text":"From our experience, the number of incorporated QDs per cell mainly is proportional to the pouring pulse voltage, length (duration) and repetition. At the same time increase in these values leads to the overall decreased cell survival.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"adkgv","text":"Another improvement can come from increasing QD concentration in the final electroporation solution. For example, by reducing Ringer's volume. But in this case air discharge can happen if electrodes are not immersed in solution.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7a83l","text":"18mm coverslips and 12 well cell culture plates can be also used.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"d8fiu","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2jubn","text":"General advices if using different electroporator:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"c5g90","text":"Specific CUY900-13-3-5 cell-culture-plate electrode has 5 mm distance between electrodes. What is important, is voltage per cm value. In our case, for pouring voltage V=200 V, l=0.5 cm, i.e. V/l = 400 V/cm.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Plate COS-7 cells using 24 or 25 mm coverslips and 6-well Corning Costar plates (Sigma #CLS3516) . Perform this step one day in advance for non-functionalized QD electroporation. If using GFP-proteins transfection, plate them 2 days in advance and 1 day in advance transfect them to allow GFP-protein expression.

One day in advance prepare mix of QD with biotinylated GFP-nanobody (bio-VHH_GFP): Per each cell coverslip/well prepare one vial with: - 2 µL of Qdot 625 streptavidin conjugate (Thermo Fisher #A10196, stock concentration 1 µM) - 20 µL of bio-VHH_GFP (purified, concentration 0.7-0.8 µg/µl) - 187 µL of PBS In case of electroporation without GFP transfection, prepare 2 µL of Qdot 625 with 198µL PBS mix immediately before step 5. Instead of biotinylated GFP-nanobody purification, one can perform biotinylation of commercially available GFP-nanobody (Chromotek gt-250) http://www.chromotek.com/products/nano-traps/gfp-trap/gfp-binding-protein/ PBS - Phosphate-Buffered Saline (10X) pH 7.4 Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog #AM9625 Qdot 625 Streptavidin Conjugate Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog #A10196
Incubate vials for 1 hour at room temperature. 01:00:00

Pre-heat Ringer's solution (10 mM Hepes, 155 mM NaCl, 1 mM CaCl2, 1 mM MgCl2, 2 mM NaH2PO4, 10 mM glucose, pH 7.2) at 37C water bath. In the absence of Ringer's, cell culture medium or PBS can be used, but with lower electroporation efficiency. In one well of new 6-well Corning plate add 1.8 ml of warm Ringer’s solution and 200 µl of QD - GFP-nanobody mix. It is convenient to put 70% ethanol and water in the neighbor well to wash electrode.

We used NEPA 21 electroporator http://www.nepagene.jp/e_products_nepagene_0001.html with CUY900-13-3-5 cell-culture-plate electrode http://www.nepagene.jp/e_products_nepagene_0008.html Setup electric pulse parameters: For poring pulse: Voltage (V) 200Length (ms) 5 Interval (ms) 50 Number 2 Decay Rate (%) 10 For transfer pulse: Voltage (V) 50Length (ms) 50Interval (ms) 10Number 10Decay Rate (%) 40 Make sure your electrode is not covered with rust, clean it.
With tweezers remove a coverslip with cells and put it into plate with well with warm Ringer's and QD-nanobody mix.

Insert electrode into well with cells coverslip, immersing it in Ringer's solution, so it touches the coverslip. Press Ω button to measure impedance. Measured impedance will be displayed in the Impedance indow (in kΩ). Make sure that it is less then 1 kΩ (0.1-0.2 is fine). If values of impedance around 30-60 kΩ, add more RInger's solution. This step is to make sure that all surfaces of electrode are fully immersed into water and electroporator will not discharge into air.

Once impedance is below threshold, press the "Start" button (or pedal, if it is equipped) and hold electrode still for the program to finish (1-2 seconds). During charging and pulsing, a warning beep will sound. When program is complete, a short double beep will sound.

Rotate electrode 90 degrees around its axis, measure impedance (step 8) and run electroporation program again ('Start' button/pedal). This step sends charge in the perpendicular direction, increasing electroporation efficiency.
Using tweezers, move cells to an empty well of 6-well plate and wash it 3-4 times with warm PBS or cell culture medium to remove QD in solution.
Wash electrode in neighbor wells (or any other volume) in water, ethanol and another round of water. Dry it with a napkin and wrap in plastic to prevent rusting.

Mount cells in any live-imaging chamber and they are ready for microscope stage! Maximum mobility is usually reached in 15-20 minutes, since QDs need to penetrate outer actin level (see paper for details).
For the next well with cells use another (fresh) vial of QD/nanobody mix.
authors: Paul M van Bergen en Henegouwen
creator: Kapitein Lab
